title: Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary
tagline: Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Gujarat

overview.short: Balaram Ambaji Wildlife Sanctuary is located at Banaskantha, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: Balaram Ambaji Wildlife Sanctuary is located at Banaskantha, Gujarat, India. It covers 542 km2, and falls in the catchment area of Banas and Sabarmati rivers, and is a part of the Khathiar-Gir dry deciduous forests' ecoregion. The sanctuary borders Rajasthan and close to mount abu. The period from October to May is considered to be the best time to visit.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (130 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (109 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Balaram Ambaji Widelife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
